hints.text: **Cyrillic — but very often paired with Latin** transliterations; **Ъ** only Bulgarian & (rarely) Russian; street = улица (ул.)

shared_with.diff: Latin next to Cyrillic + EU plates → Bulgaria; frequent Ъ as a vowel → Bulgarian.
Macedonian Ѕ Ј Љ Њ → North Macedonia.

hints.text: Gen4 cars: blue (pan-European), **red** (also LV/LT/RU/SK/SE) or black (also GR/HR) — ± antenna, sometimes hidden; Gen3 = mostly **March/April dead-'winter'** look + grey-tape **'flag antenna'**
Cylindrical concrete poles (+ladder variant, wooden); most poletops = small **alternating hook insulators**; **3 large upward-hook insulators ≈ unique to Bulgaria** (downward variant exists)
hints.uniq_note: The 3-large-hook poletop is 'probably unique' per Plonk It — the small alternating hooks are the everyday version.

hints.text: Chevrons red-on-white (also RO/TR/MK; GR/RS = black-white); directional + town signs show **two lines (Cyrillic + Latin)** — visible even from afar
hints.uniq_note: The two-line bilingual signage is the fast Bulgaria tell among Cyrillic countries.

hints.text: Pedestrian sign: **4 stripes + person wearing a hat** — no other Eastern/Central-European sign has 4 stripes
Bollards **rare** & generic (red front/white back, inconsistent shape — ≈ HR/HU designs)
**Thin road lines** (Romania: much thicker); road quality fairly poor (cracks, potholes)
Orange tiled roofs w/ **mortar patches along corner tiles** — famously uneven, 'poorly built' look
hints.uniq_note: The sloppy mortar-corner roofs are the classic Bulgaria architecture tell.

hints.text: **Metallic trash bins** of a distinct shape (also TR/RS/AL/MK)
**Balkan range splits the country**: flat north to Romania; **Rhodope** SE w/ highest peaks; second flat area between the ranges; west = small valleys in hills
**Area codes ordered by first digit** — the single best urban region clue
**Pirin & Rila NP**: pyramid peaks visible from Blagoevgrad/Kyustendil/Sofia provinces; wind turbines → Dobrich coast; Alpine-look houses → Smolyan region

**Car metas**: invisible Gen4 car → N+W borders + northern coast; red Gen4 → central + SE; short tape antenna → east (never Vratsa/Montana/Pazardzhik/Smolyan); taped LONG antenna + white car → Stara Zagora (+rare Plovdiv/Burgas); bulky unpeeled-tape antenna + March coverage → **Varna**
**Vratsa**: distinct 'bumpy' mountains SW of the city; **Nessebar/Sozopol** old towns = unpainted stone + white walls + brown wood + cobblestone
**Sofia**: white street signs w/ two red strips (others exist); **Stara Zagora**: brown signs w/ bright yellow text
